pr_summary: This PR adds new functionality to the codebase.
verdict_reason: The review found critical-severity issues requiring attention.

findings.line_content: await db.query('UPDATE users SET password = ? WHERE email = ?', [newPassword, email]);
findings.title: Plaintext Password Storage
findings.description: The password is being stored in plaintext, which is a critical security vulnerability. An attacker who gains access to the database can obtain all user passwords. The password should be hashed before being stored.
findings.suggestion: Use a password hashing library like bcrypt to hash the password before storing it: await db.query('UPDATE users SET password = ? WHERE email = ?', [bcrypt.hashSync(newPassword, 10), email]);

findings.title: Missing test: getUsers with null or empty ids
findings.description: This test is important because it would catch a potential crash or incorrect behavior when the ids parameter is null, undefined, or empty. It would also verify that the function handles this case correctly.
findings.suggestion: Test with null, undefined, and empty string inputs for the ids parameter, and verify that the function returns an empty array or a meaningful error message.

findings.title: Sequential Async Operations
findings.description: The loop awaits an async function on each iteration, resulting in sequential execution. This can be optimized by using Promise.all() for parallel execution.
findings.suggestion: Replace the loop with Promise.all() to execute the async operations in parallel

findings.title: Missing test: getUserActivity with null or empty userId
findings.description: This test is important because it would catch a potential crash or incorrect behavior when the userId parameter is null, undefined, or empty. It would also verify that the function handles this case correctly.
findings.suggestion: Test with null, undefined, and empty string inputs for the userId parameter, and verify that the function returns a meaningful error message or handles the error correctly.

findings.line_content: const enriched = logs.map(log => { return { ...log, user: users[log.user_id] }; });
findings.title: Unvalidated User Input
findings.description: The user_id field in the logs is not validated or sanitized before being used to access the users array. This could lead to errors or unexpected behavior if the input is malformed. The input should be validated and sanitized before being used.
findings.suggestion: Validate and sanitize the user_id field: const enriched = logs.map(log => { return { ...log, user: users[parseInt(log.user_id)] }; });

findings.line_content: const logs = await db.query('SELECT * FROM activity_logs WHERE user_id = ?', [userId]);
findings.title: Missing Pagination
findings.description: The query fetches all matching rows without a LIMIT clause, which can cause performance issues if the table grows large.
findings.suggestion: Add a LIMIT clause to the query to paginate the results

positive_observations: The code follows a consistent structure.
Function naming is clear and descriptive.
missing_tests: This test is important because it would catch a potential crash or incorrect behavior when the ids parameter is null, undefined, or empty. It would also verify that the function handles this case correctly.

This test is important because it would catch a potential crash or incorrect behavior when the userId parameter is null, undefined, or empty. It would also verify that the function handles this case correctly.
